zh.Spaldington, Yorkshire, England: 英國越可爾斯帕爾丁頓
zh.lifeSummary: 當Thomas de la Hay於大約1315年出生在英國越可爾斯帕爾丁頓時，他的父親Peter de la Hay I22歲，母親Cecilia19歲。他與Agnes le Botiller結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1379年12月21日在他的家鄉享年65歲。

zh-hans.Spaldington, Yorkshire, England: 英国越可爾斯帕爾丁頓
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Thomas de la Hay于大约1315年出生在英国越可爾斯帕爾丁頓时，他的父亲Peter de la Hay I22岁，母亲Cecilia19岁。他与Agnes le Botiller结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他去世于1379年12月21日在他的家乡享年65岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: la
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: Hay
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish and English (northern; of Norman origin): habitational name from any of several places in Normandy called La Haie or La Haye (Old French haie ‘hedge, enclosure’, ‘forest for hunting deer and other animals’, a borrowing of the ancient Germanic word haga). Robert de Haia or de la Haye is known to have come from La Haye-du-Puits in Manche; he was the founder of Boxgrove Priory in Sussex (1123), and holder of the Honor of Halnaker (Sussex) and (by marriage) the barony of Kolswein (Lincolnshire). The Norman name was also taken to Ireland, where it has since flourished in the county of Wexford as Hay and Hayes . Elsewhere in Ireland the name usually has a native Irish origin, see below.

English: topographic name from Middle English hay(e), heye, heghe ‘enclosure’ (Old English (ge)hæg) or ‘forest fenced off for hunting’ (Old French haie); or else a habitational name from a place so called, such as Hay (in Herefordshire and Westmorland) or Hey in Scammonden (Yorkshire). It was no doubt sometimes synonymous with Hayward .
English: nickname for a tall man, from Middle English heigh, hey, high ‘high, tall’ (Old English hēah).

variants.name: High
meanings: English:
nickname for a tall man, from Middle English heigh, hey, high ‘high, tall’ (Old English hēah). Compare Hay 3.
topographic name for someone who lived ‘(on the) height or hill’, from Middle English heigh ‘height, hilltop’ (Old English hēah). Compare Hight .

variants.name: Hai
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Arabic/Muslim Abdul, Syed, Arifa, Atta, Faiz, Hamid, Iftekhar, Iskandar, Mohamed, Mohammad, Mohannad.  Vietnamese Mui, Pham, Tran, Vo, Bui, Cao, Chau, Dinh, Duong, Nguyen, Sau, Vo Van.  Jewish Ilan, Ronen, Shlomo.  Chinese Chan, Chiming, Lung, Wai Fun, Yin.
Muslim (mainly the Indian subcontinent): from a personal name based on Arabic ḥayy ‘alive’. Al-Ḥayy ‘the Living’ is an attribute of Allah.
Jewish: variant of   Hay  .

variants.name: Dehay
meanings: French: topographic name, with fused preposition de ‘from’, for someone who lived by an enclosure, from the masculine form of Old French haye ‘hedge’; or a habitational name from any of various places called with this word (see Hay 9).
Altered form (also De Hay) of Dutch De Haij or De Haaij, which is probably of French origin (see above).

variants.name: Haig
meanings: Scottish and Irish (of Norman origin): habitational name from any of various places in northern France named with Old Norse hagi ‘enclosure’, a word with cognates in most ancient Germanic languages. Compare Hay . The Haigs of Bemersyde in Berwickshire derive their name from the district of La Hague in Manche.
Scottish and Irish (Cavan): shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Thaoig and Mac Thaidhg (see McCaig ).

variants.name: Hayman
meanings: English: topographic name probably from Middle English hai(e), hei(e) ‘enclosure’ (Old English (ge)hæg), denoting a dweller by an enclosure (see Hay 2 ), or else a nickname for a ‘keeper of the enclosure’ (compare Hayward ).
English: nickname for a tall man, from Middle English heigh, high(e) (Old English hēah); see also Hay 3.
English: occupational name for a seller of hay, from Middle English hai, hei (Old English hēg, hīg).

variants.name: Hayes
meanings: Irish (Cork): shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó hAodha ‘descendant of Aodh’, a personal name meaning ‘fire’. Compare McCoy . In some cases especially in County Wexford, the surname is of English origin (see below), having been taken to Ireland by the Normans.
English: variant of Hay , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

English: topographic name from the plural form of Middle English hay(e), heye, heghe ‘enclosure’ (see Hay ), sometimes used as a collective noun for a farm, especially in Devon, where it is a frequent minor placename. Compare Hain .

variants.name: Haycock
meanings: English: from a pet form of the Middle English personal name Hai + coc; see Hay 4 and compare Cocke .

variants.name: Haylett
meanings: English (Norfolk): from a pet form (double diminutive) of the personal name Hai (see Hay 4), from Hai + -el + -ot.

variants.name: Hey
meanings: English (Yorkshire and Lancashire): variant of   Hay  .
Dutch: variant, mostly Americanized (or archaic) and Flemish, of Heij, a topographic name for someone who lived on a heath, Dutch hei, heide.
German: metonymic occupational name for a grower or mower of grass, from Middle High German höu ‘grass, hay’.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Thomas de la Hay was born about 1315, in Spaldington, Yorkshire, England, his father, Peter de la Hay I, was 22 and his mother, Cecilia, was 19. He married Agnes le Botiller. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died on 21 December 1379, in his hometown, at the age of 65.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Thomas de la Hay est né environ 1315, à Spaldington, Everwyck, Angleterre, son père, Peter de la Hay I, avait 22 ans et sa mère, Cecilia, avait 19 ans. Il a épousé Agnes le Botiller. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé le 21 décembre 1379, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 65 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Thomas de la Hay um 1315 in Spaldington, Yorkshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Peter de la Hay I 22 und seine Mutter Cecilia 19. Er hat Agnes le Botiller geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb am 21. Dezember 1379 im Alter von 65 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Thomas de la Hay è nato il circa 1315 nel Spaldington, Yorkshire, England, il suo padre, Peter de la Hay I, aveva 22 anni e il suo madre, Cecilia, aveva 19 anni. Lui sposato Agnes le Botiller. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì il 21 dicembre 1379, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 65 anni.

ja.Wortley, Yorkshire, England: イギリスヨークシュアイアウオルトリー
ja.Spaldington, Yorkshire, England: イギリスヨークシュアイアスプアルディントン
ja.lifeSummary: Thomas de la Hayが約1315年にイギリスヨークシュアイアスプアルディントンで生まれた時，父Peter de la Hay Iは22歳，母Ceciliaは19歳であった。Agnes le Botillerと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼は1379年12月21日に故郷で65歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Thomas de la Hay(이)가 대략 1315년 영국 요르크실르 스팔딩턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Peter de la Hay I(은)는 22세였고, 그의 어머니 Cecilia(은)는 19세였다. 그는 Agnes le Botiller(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1379년 12월 21일 그의 고향에서 65세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Thomas de la Hay nasceu aproximadamente 1315, em Spaldington, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Peter de la Hay I, tinha 22 anos e sua mãe, Cecilia, tinha 19 anos. Ele casou-se com Agnes le Botiller. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu em 21 de dezembro de 1379, em sua cidade natal, com 65 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Thomas de la Hay родился около 1315 г. в местности спалдингтон, еркшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Peter de la Hay I было 22 года, а матери, которую звали Cecilia, 19. Он женился на женщине по имени Agnes le Botiller. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер 21 декабря 1379 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 65 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Thomas de la Hay nació aproximadamente 1315, en Spaldington, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Peter de la Hay I, tenía 22 años y su mamá, Cecilia, tenía 19 años. Se casó con Agnes le Botiller. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió el 21 de diciembre de 1379, en su pueblo, a la edad de 65 años.
